user_query:
I’ve exported a Logseq page to input/logseq_page.md. Please parse it and convert the bullets into slipbox-ready notes with proper source metadata. Do this in two steps:

1) Precheck: Read input/logseq_page.md, extract the page properties (title, author, source/type), and scan all bullets. Generate a brief 1–2 sentence summary of the page, and count how many notes will be created (each top-level bullet becomes a note; include nested bullets as standalone notes with parent context). Write the precheck file to output/precheck.json with this exact JSON structure:
{
  "summary": "...",
  "note_count": <integer>,
  "source": {
    "type": "...",
    "title": "...",
    "author": "..."
  }
}
Do not create notes yet in this step.

2) Import (assume I confirm): Convert the bullets into individual notes and write them to output/notes.txt, one note per line, using this exact format:
- {content} ~ {source.type}, {source.title} by {source.author}

Parsing rules you must follow:
- Ignore all Logseq tags and metadata: remove inline #tags, block-tags:: lines/segments, id:: metadata, and any tags:: property. Strip the leading # in values like type:: #literature. Do not carry over alias:: or status::.
- Convert [[page refs]] to plain text (keep the words, drop the brackets).
- Nested bullets must be flattened into standalone notes by adding parent context. Use the pattern: "ParentTitle (parent detail) Child content" where the parent’s text before a colon is the title and the text after the colon is the parent detail. For deeply nested bullets, accumulate context from all ancestors in the same manner.
- Treat non-bullet body text and any "## Citation:" section as non-importable (ignore them entirely).
- Preserve other markdown formatting if present (bold, italic, code), but do not include any tags.

Write only the two files: output/precheck.json and output/notes.txt. Do not write anywhere else.
